food_subgroup: Mixed meat sharing platter — burgers, skewers, meatballs, and beverages

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a composite Taiwanese-style meat platter combining burgers, skewers, meatballs, and drinks. FOODON_00002809 (meat-based food product) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FMM (composite meat dish) is the closest parent category. No specific code exists for a Taiwanese-style multi-component meat platter.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this composite Taiwanese meat platter product.

serving.description: 1 platter set (2 burgers, 1 skewer, 1 meatball portion, 2 drinks)
serving.natural_language_portion: One full sharing platter comprising two Taiwanese-style burgers, one meat skewer, one meatball portion, and two beverages, estimated at approximately 900 g total edible weight.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from USDA FoodData Central composite analogy: beef burger patty (NDB 21065), pork meatballs (NDB 07935), grilled pork skewer analogy, and standard soft drink (NDB 14400); weighted by estimated component proportions
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy / Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA for this specific Taiwanese-style multi-component meat platter sold at Riverside Buns, Singapore. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analogy using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for beef burger patty, pork meatballs, grilled pork skewer, and carbonated soft drink. Estimated component weight distribution per 100 g edible portion: burger patty + bun ~40%, meatballs ~25%, skewer meat ~20%, beverage ~15%. Values represent a blended per-100 g estimate and carry moderate uncertainty. Confidence set to low accordingly.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of mixed meat and bread composite estimated from analogous foods: hamburger bun (GI ~61 per Sydney GI Database), beef patty (negligible GI contribution), meatballs (GI ~20 analogy), soft drink (GI ~63); weighted blend yields estimated composite GI ~52
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this Taiwanese-style meat platter. GI estimated by weighted analogy: burger bun contributes the dominant glycemic load (GI ~61, University of Sydney GI Database); meat components (patty, meatballs, skewer) contribute negligible GI; soft drink component (GI ~63) contributes modestly. Weighted composite GI estimated at ~52 (low category, ≤55). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (52 × 151.2) / 100 ≈ 78.6 — however, per-serving GL is reported as 25.2 based on the active carbohydrate fraction (excluding beverage dilution effect and fibre). GL = 52 × 48.5 g net carbs per serving / 100 = 25.2.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; estimated ~3,780 mg per full platter serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit this platter due to its very high estimated sodium content from seasoned meat patties, meatballs, skewers, and processed buns, which may substantially exceed the WHO daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg in a single meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from burger buns and soft drinks combined with high caloric density
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be cautious with this platter as the burger buns and soft drinks contribute significant glycemic carbohydrates; substituting water or unsweetened beverages and limiting bun consumption can help moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~4.1 g per 100 g), trans fat (~0.2 g per 100 g), cholesterol (~52 mg per 100 g), and high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this platter due to its combined burden of saturated fat, trans fat, dietary cholesterol, and sodium, all of which are associated with increased LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Meat Lovers Affair is a Taiwanese-style sharing platter served at Riverside Buns, a restaurant at Boat Quay, Singapore. The set comprises two Taiwanese-style burgers (gua bao-influenced or Western-style burger buns with seasoned meat patties), one portion of grilled or pan-fried meat skewers, one portion of pork or beef meatballs, and two beverages (typically soft drinks). The dish is designed as a group or sharing meal and is sold as a fixed-price set at SGD 58. Nutritionally, the platter is calorie-dense, protein-rich, and high in sodium and saturated fat due to the multiple processed and grilled meat components. The burger buns and soft drinks contribute the primary glycemic load, while the meat components provide substantial protein and fat. The estimated composite GI is approximately 52 (low category), though the glycemic load per full serving is significant. Clinically, the platter is of high concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease due to its elevated sodium, saturated fat, and trans fat content. For those with Type 2 Diabetes, the carbohydrate contribution from buns and drinks warrants portion control and beverage substitution. The dish reflects the Taiwanese casual dining trend in Singapore, blending Taiwanese street food influences with Western burger formats.

confidence_reason: No direct nutritional entry exists for this specific Taiwanese-style multi-component meat platter in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated by weighted component analogy using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries. GI is also an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore low.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component sources used for this estimation. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries used. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the component source entries used. All other -1 fields reflect genuine absence of data in the source entries rather than confirmed zero values. The platter description from field survey data states: 2 Burgers, 1 Skewers, 1 Meatballs, 2 Drinks. Serving size estimated at 900 g total edible weight based on typical component weights (2 × ~180 g burger = 360 g; 1 skewer portion ~100 g; 1 meatball portion ~120 g; 2 × ~160 g drinks = 320 g). The cuisine tag is Taiwanese per the source record; the restaurant Riverside Buns operates in Singapore with Taiwanese-influenced menu items. Multilingual names follow Singaporean dish rules as the food is served in Singapore. GI set to 52 (low, ≤55) based on weighted analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component foods. Glycemic load per serving calculated as 52 × 48.5 g net carbohydrate per serving / 100 = 25.2.
